retention.reason: retained archive would exceed the structured-file inventory max_bytes guardrail; closing after distillation instead
retention.rule: Retained closeout evidence preserves reportable closeout facts when the full execplan archive exceeds inventory size guardrails.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Implement #1367 compositional operation IR and modular primitive architecture.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep scope inside existing operation IR, generated command package, primitive runtime support, docs/tests, and the companion command-generation package; do not invent a new dashboard/state store.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Choose the representative migrated operation, exact proof, and whether small generator support belongs in command-generation.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: Escalate if closing #1367 requires a broader generated CLI migration or removing all remaining runtime primitive debt.

execution_run.what happened: Implemented operation-local fragments in the operation schema and config.report, added generated Python operation_composition helpers from command-generation, added TypeScript fragment expansion support, updated fragment-aware validators/checkers, documented the IR ladder, and added focused tests.
execution_run.scope touched: operation/schema contracts; command-generation dependency pin; generated Python/TypeScript targets; checkers; docs; focused tests; planning evidence
execution_run.changed surfaces: operation/schema contracts; command-generation dependency; generated Python/TypeScript targets; TypeScript runtime support; checkers; docs; tests; planning evidence
execution_run.validations run: command-generation tests passed after rebase; AW generated-package proof with Node passed; generated freshness check passed; focused fragment tests passed; structured inventory passed.
execution_run.result for continuation: #1367 ready for PR; #1364 remains separate broader migration work.
execution_run.next step: Open PR and link companion command-generation PR #7.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; this closes #1367 without claiming full #1364 migration.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes; representative full path covers schema, operation, generated Python/TypeScript execution support, modular helper split, docs, and checks.
finished_run_review.config compliance: AW start, implement, planning delegation-decision, proof, summary, and required validation were used.
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low after PR body and pin update call out #1364 boundary and command-generation PR #7 dependency.
finished_run_review.follow-on decision: #1364 remains open; no new follow-up issue found in this lane.

proof_report.validation proof: command-generation tests passed after rebase; AW generated-package proof with Node passed; generated freshness check passed; focused fragment tests passed; structured inventory passed.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Generated package proof with Node, focused tests, contract tooling, structured inventory, schema docs, maintainer surfaces, workspace tests, and lint all passed.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Implement #1367 compositional operation IR and modular primitive architecture.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Operation schema has ir_step/ir_fragment, config.report uses select-and-emit-report, command-generation PR #7 provides generic fragment expansion and operation_composition module, AW generated Python/TypeScript targets are fresh, checkers expand fragments, docs describe the ladder, and focused/full proof passed.
intent_satisfaction.unsolved intent passed to: #1364 for broader generated CLI runtime migration only.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.
closure_check.why this decision is honest: #1367 compositional IR is complete, but the broader #1364 generated CLI runtime migration remains open and is not claimed by this PR.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: Focused tests, generated-package proof with Node, generated freshness check, contract tooling, structured inventory, schema docs, maintainer surfaces, workspace tests, and lint.
closure_check.reopen trigger: A generated target cannot execute a uses_fragment plan, fragments bypass primitive support checks, or operation composition returns to a monolithic primitive executor only.

durable_residue.learned constraint: Operation composition belongs in operation-local fragments before broadening primitive vocabulary.
durable_residue.motivation worth preserving: Prevents broad hand-owned primitives from hiding ordinary reusable dataflow.
durable_residue.canonical owner now: operation.schema.json, operation_primitives.json, command-generation operation_composition, and contributor playbook.
durable_residue.promotion trigger: Promote to Memory only if future agents repeatedly miss the primitive/fragment/operation/command ladder.

execution_summary.validation confirmed: command-generation tests passed after rebase; AW generated-package proof with Node passed; generated freshness check passed; focused fragment tests passed; structured inventory passed.
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: #1364 broader generated CLI runtime migration remains open.
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: PR body, schema-shaped grounding review, closeout evidence, docs, and tests.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): Docs: contributor playbook operation IR ladder. No Memory note needed; durable facts are in contracts/tests/docs.
execution_summary.resume from: Review/merge #1367 PR, then continue #1364 if prioritized.

continuation.summary: #1364 remains the broader generated CLI runtime migration owner.

memory.summary: No Memory note needed; durable guidance is in contracts/docs/tests.
